segment-text: Anne Rosellini
Based on the novel WINTER'S BONE By Daniel Woodrell
Opening montage
Wide shot of a wintry hollow with a lone trailer and an American flag flying. SONNY, a boy of 12, and his sister, ASHLEE, 6, bounce on trampoline, play with kittens, and horse around on a skateboard.

(sings) Way down in Missouri when I heard this melody, When I was just a little baby on my mama's knee The old folks were humming, the banjos were strumming So sweet and low. Hushabye my baby, go to sleep on mama's knee Journey back to these old hills in dreams again with me It seems like your mama was there once again And the old folks were strumming
that same old refrain
Ashlee helps her sister, REE, 17, hang laundry. Ree buttons Ashlee's doll's dress, hands it to her sister, and then picks up the full laundry basket.
(sings) Way down in Missouri where I heard this lullaby, When the stars were blinking and the moon was shining high and I hear mama calling as in days long ago Singing hushabye. Let's go.
TITLE: WINTER'S BONE (OVER IMAGE OF TREE BRANCHES) INT. REE'S HOUSE - MORNING
Ree splashes her face in the kitchen sink and dries it on a towel. Radio plays softly in the background. Across the room, Ashlee is sleeping under a blanket on the couch.
Ashlee pounces on her brother sleeping in the chair.
Wake up!

Ashlee pounces again. Ree drops fat into a hot skillet. She cuts a potato and drops the pieces into the hissing fat. The radio music becomes a newscast reporting the cold weather conditions. Ashlee, wrapped in a blanket, watches Ree at the stove. Morning.
Morning.
Ree opens the fridge and pokes around for something to give the dog. She takes out a bowl, smells what's inside and hands it to Ashlee.
Ashlee heads outside to give the dog his food.
EXT. REE'S HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
Ashlee carries the dog's food across the yard. NICKDOG, the family hound, greets her enthusiastically. She places the bowl on the ground.
INT. REE'S HOUSE - BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
Ree combs her mother's wet hair. A hymn plays on the radio. Sonny enters, holding a dog.
Peanut Butter. Where'd you find Peanut Butter?
In the woods. He's sweet. Ree and her mother pet the dog.
EXT. DIRT ROAD - DAY
Ree, Sonny, Ashlee and two dogs are walking to school.
A-woo.
Uh uh.
Ssssss.
S - E.
H - O - U - S - E.
Good.
INT. SCHOOL, CHILDREN'S CLASSROOM - DAY
Ashlee does an art project at a table with other children. Ree watches her and then walks down the hallway to the high school wing of the K-12 school.
INT. SCHOOL, TEEN CLASSROOM - DAY
A parenting class. The STUDENTS each hold a mechanical baby and follow the TEACHER'S instructions. Ree watches from the hallway.
Transfer. Careful. Cradle the head. And then take him to your
shoulder. So he's up a little bit
Shots of the teens in the class earnestly trying to hold their model babies correctly.
INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY - DAY
ROTC students march down hallway with rifles and turn to enter the gym. Ree approaches from the opposite direction. Could I have your attention, please? Forsyth Misouri chapter of
One of Ree's former classmates sees her and waves.
in Mrs. Blair's room. Front and Center.
INT SCHOOL HALLWAY - DAY
From a doorway we see ROTC students drilling in the gym. Ree watches them through the window. Truman University will be here next Monday at 8:45.
Ree signs up at a clipboard. EXT. BLOND MILTON'S YARD - DAY
SONYA, Ree's relative, is feeding her horses in her barn area. Sonya and her husband live across the holler from Ree's family.
(To the horses) Alright.
EXT. REE'S YARD - DAY
Ree leads a horse across a wintry yard to Sonya's barn area.
EXT. BLOND MILTON'S YARD - DAY
Ree walks towards the barn.
Hey Ree.
Sonya puts down the pitchfork and faces Ree.
You and the kids alright? We're just a little short on cash right now.
How long's it been since the horse ate? About four days.
That hay gets kind of expensive, don't it? Yes ma'am. (beat) Actually, I was wondering if you could keep her with yours?
After a long pause, Sonya opens the gate to the pasture and ushers Ree's horse in to join the others.
(to Ree's horse) Alright, Ginger, Come on in. Come on. There you go. Have a little bit of that. Come on. Get going. Thank you, Sonya.
Ree takes a last look at her horse and retraces her steps across the yard.
EXT. REE'S YARD - DAY

Ree chops wood. She's got headphones on and the music is fueling her chopping. There is the sound of a car approaching. Nickdog barks and runs across the yard to greet the car. Ree's relatives, Sonya and her husband, BLOND MILTON, are skinning a deer in their yard. They look up to see who's approaching. As the car gets closer, Ree can see that it is a police car. She throws down her axe and approaches the car as it pulls to a stop. SHERIFF BASKIN, 30's, gets out. Ree climbs the porch steps and blocks the front door of her house.
EXT. REE'S HOUSE, PORCH - DAY
How are you today?
Ree doesn't answer.
Ask me inside. I need to talk some with your mama.
Ree opens the door and steps aside.
Sheriff Baskin enters the house.
Ma'am? I was wondering if I could ask you a few questions. (beat) Ma'am?
Ree can hear that her mother is not responding. Baskin returns to the porch.
Baskin positions himself directly across from Ree and leans against the porch post.
You know your daddy's out on bond, don't you? So what?
Looks like he's been cooking again. I know that's the charges you laid on him, but you ain't proved it on him. You got to prove it every time.
Well, that won't be no hard thing to do. But that ain't even why I'm here. His court date's next week, and I can't seem to turn him up. Maybe he sees you and ducks.
That could be. But where you all come into this is, he put this house here, and your timber acres up for his bond. He what now?
Jessup signed over everything. If he doesn't show at trial, see, the way the deal works is, you all gonna lose this place. (MORE)
(beat) You got some place to go?
Shock courses through Ree, but she tries to mask it.
Girl, I've been lookin'. I said I'll find him.
Baskin looks out across the property. He sees Blond Milton scoping him out. After a moment Baskin turns to leave.
You make sure your daddy knows the gravity of this deal.
Baskin drives away. Blond Milton and Sonya resume skinning their deer. Sonny carries split wood to the porch and stands next to Ree, watching Sonya and Blond cut down the hanging meat in their yard.
Maybe they'll share some of that with us. That could be.
Maybe we should ask.
Ree looks at her little brother, holding his chin in her hand.
INT. REE'S HOUSE - EVENING
Ashlee and Sonny are warming themselves by the wood stove. Sonya enters, carrying in a large cardboard box that has a venison bone jutting above the rim.
Evening, Ree. Evening.
Kids. Hey, Connie. Didn't want you to think we'd forgot y'all.
Sonya sets the box on the counter.
Brought you some meat, some potatoes, and some stuff for a good stew, anyway. Thank you.
We can use it.
Seen your woodpile's low out front. We got that splitter up there in the yard. You should use it. I'll do that.
We seen the law was out here this afternoon. Everything alright?
He's hunting for Dad. Sonny, listening to the conversation, looks up.
Hunting Jessup is he? where he's at? No.
You sure about that? Yes. You know
Alright, alright, well I guess you didn't have nothing to tell him, then, did you? I wouldn't tell him nothing if I did.
Oh, we know that. I'm gonna let you get to it, alright?
Sonya leaves abruptly, waving goodbye to Connie and pulling the door shut behind her. Ree looks at her brother and sister.
Mmm hmm.
Alright, both of you need to get over here and watch how I make it.
EXT. RURAL ROAD - DAY
Ree walks along a brambly road and turns off into a driveway.
EXT. GAIL'S HOUSE, PORCH - DAY
Ree crosses the yard and climbs a porch. She knocks on the door and stands, waiting. She can hear thrash metal music coming from inside. The door is opened by GAIL, 17, holding her baby NED.
Thank god it's you, Sweet Pea, and not Floyd's mommy and daddy again. Them two watch me like I done something wrong.
INT. GAIL'S HOUSE, LIVING ROOM - DAY
FLOYD, 18, is pulling on the boots to his ATV outfit.
Would you hush your mouth about them. They put a roof over your head ain't they? Hey, Floyd. You gonna invite me in? Or I could just stay out here and talk to you.
Yeah, you can come in.
Ree steps inside the house and walks past Floyd to the
kitchen. INT. GAIL'S HOUSE, KITCHEN - DAY
Gail holds Ned. She hugs Ree.
Hey.
Ree takes in Ned and the fact that her best friend now has a baby.
Don't start. Come on.
Gail carries Ned down the narrow hall to the bedroom. follows.
INT. GAIL'S HOUSE, BEDROOM - DAY
Ree flops down on the bed. Gail lies down next to her, putting Ned between them.
It's been a while, Sweet Pea. Things stack up is all.
What's going on? The sheriff came by looking for my Dad. If he don't show up for his court date, we're gonna lose the house. Gotta get down to the Arkansas line.
I gotta ask him.
It's his truck. Gail gets up and leaves the room. Ree holds Ned while Gail talks to Floyd. Gail returns.
He said no. Did you tell him I'd spring for gas?
I told him. He still won't. Why not?
He never says why not to me, Ree. He just says no.
Man, that's so sad to hear you say he won't let you do something, and then you don't do it.
It's different once you're married.
Gail flops back onto the bed.
It really must be. Cause you ain't never used to eat no shit.
Ned needs his nap.
Gail takes Ned from Ree's arms.
(whispers to baby) Come here.
Ree leaves and Gail watches her go.
EXT. WOODS - DAY
Ree treks up a wooded hill.
EXT. TEARDROP'S YARD - DAY
Ree enters the yard, greeted by dogs. VICTORIA, 30's, opens the door of a rough-hewn brown painted cabin.
What brings you here? dead?
I'm looking for my Dad.
Victoria holds the door for Ree and beckons her inside.
Come on in. Shake off the chill.
Ree enters the house, followed by Victoria.
INT. TEARDROP'S HOUSE, KITCHEN - DAY
Ree and Victoria enter kitchen.
Let's keep it quiet. Teardrop's still laying up in bed.
Ree takes off her coat and sits down at the table. Victoria pours two cups of coffee.
Victoria, I really gotta run Dad down to get him to show. Ree's uncle, TEARDROP, 40's, a worn, unshaven man enters. lights a cigarette and crosses the room.
You ought not do that. Don't go running after Jessup.
Teardrop sits at the table.
Show or don't show that choice is up to the one going to jail, not you. You know where he's at, don't you?
Where a man's at ain't necessarily for you to know neither. But you do...
Teardrop abruptly shoots a warning look at Ree.
I ain't seen him.
Victoria places a cup of coffee in front of him. while Ree contemplates her next move. Silence
Teardrop absentmindedly loads the magazine of a pistol.

Don't you ever go down around Little Arthur's asking them people about shit they ain't offering to talk about. That's a real good way to end up et by hogs, or wishing you was. We're all related ain't we?
Teardrop continues to fiddle with the pistol.
Our relations get watered kinda thin between here and Little
Arthur's.
Teardrop tosses the pistol onto the lazy susan in the middle of the table.
You know all those people, Teardrop. You could ask.
Shut up.
None of them's gonna be in a great big hurry to tangle with you.
I said shut up once already with my mouth.
Teardrop gets up and walks across the kitchen to the stove with his back to Ree.
Teardrop spins back to face her.
You think I forgot that? Hmm? Jessup and me run together for coming on forty years but I don't know where he's at, and I ain't going to go around asking after him neither.
Victoria tries to cut the tension by changing the subject.
Ree, you still planning to join the army?
No, I don't think I can any more. (to Teardrop) Listen...
Teardrop lunges at Ree, grabs her hair and pulls her face back to look at him. He leans his face in very close to hers.
(shaking his head) No.

He lets her go and exits the kitchen. Victoria locks eyes with Ree, then follows Teardrop. Ree, sitting at the table, can hear their muffled voices from the next room. She rises and puts on her coat as they argue.
She needs help.
Nope. Nope. listen. Listen! You tell that girl to stay close to home. Don't move. Give her this. You send her ass on her way right
now. Victoria comes back into the kitchen.
Teardrop says you best keep your ass real close to the willows, dear. He hopes this helps.
Victoria presses a wad of bills into Ree's hand. joint out of her cigarette package. She slips a
Here's a doobie for your walk. Ree takes the joint. Thank you.
Victoria stares at Ree as she leaves.
EXT. RURAL FIELD - LATE AFTERNOON
Ree hikes across a ruddy pasture strewn with dilapidated sheds.
EXT. RURAL ROAD - LATE AFTERNOON
Ree crosses the road and heads up a hill.
EXT. MEGAN'S YARD - LATE AFTERNOON

Ree enters a yard with laundry hanging on a line. A donkey is grazing on the other side of the fence. A half-eaten apple has rolled out of his reach. She picks it up and feeds it to the donkey. MEGAN, 20, comes out onto the porch and calls out to Ree.
Hey! What's your business here? My dad's, Jessup. He's pals with Little Arthur, and I gotta find him.
Megan stares Ree down, taking a long time to answer.
Hold on.

Megan goes inside the house. Ree takes in the details of the yard. The wind strikes up, ominous clanking sounds surround her. Megan comes back out with her coat on, approaches Ree, and guides her through the densely packed debris in the yard.
My name's Megan, and I do know you, really. Seen you at some of the reunions. I knew Jessup when I seen him, too. Never did talk with him much.
You knew him?
Knew him when I seen him around, I mean. He does stuff I hear about. He cooks crank.
They all do now. You don't even need to say it out loud.
INT. LITTLE ARTHUR'S HOUSE - AFTERNOON
LITTLE ARTHUR, mid-30s, nervously looks out his window.
EXT. LITTLE ARTHUR'S YARD - AFTERNOON
Megan and Ree approach Little Arthur's house.
If Arthur's been runnin' on crank for a day or two, you should just leave, okay?
The door opens and Little Arthur smiles at Ree.
I knew it - I been in your dreams, ain't I, little Ruthie?
It's Ree, you asshole. And I'm only out to find Dad.
Come on in, ladies.
Ree pushes past Megan and enters the house. Megan follows.
INT. LITTLE ARTHUR'S HOUSE - AFTERNOON

No fresh light makes its way through the windows. Little Arthur shows affection to his pet ferret, tapping on the glass of the tank. Ree and Megan hover near the door and watch him from the other side of the room.
He turns away from the ferret and sits on the couch.
Not since the summer. At y'all's place.
You ain't seen him nowhere since then?
Huh-uh. He kept leavin' the house goin' someplace- you don't know where?
You got cat shit in your ear, girl? I've got them two boys and Mom to tend, man. I need him.
If I see the dude, I'll tell him you said that.
Little Arthur lights a cigarette, his hands shaking.
Your dad left you to do all that? That's fucked up. Well, he had to, the way things go, you know.
Ree stares at Arthur. He lurches forward on the couch.
You want a line? No.
You wanna blow some smoke? No.
Little Arthur slaps the table and stands up.
Then I guess I got nothing for you.
He waves his hand to dismiss them.
Go on!
Ree and Megan waste no time leaving. Arthur watches them go.
EXT. LONESTAR ROAD - AFTERNOON
Megan and Ree walk up the road.
Don't tell nobody it was me who told you this, okay?
But you're going to have to go on up the hill and ask for a talk with Thump Milton. Now, I hope he'll talk with you - he generally won't. That man, scares me way more than the rest.
Well, scared ain't a bad way to be about him, neither, hon. He's my own granpaw, and I still make damn sure I never piss him off none.
They continue walking until they reach Megan's property.
EXT. MEGAN'S YARD - AFTERNOON
You go see Thump. Megan holds down a barbed wire fence to allow Ree to climb over it.
Go on.
Ree heads up the hill while Megan watches her go.
EXT. WOODS - AFTERNOON
Ree trudges through the woods. Dry leaves and branches crackle under her footsteps as she makes her way.
EXT. THUMP MILTON'S YARD - AFTERNOON
MERAB, 50's, comes out to the porch of her house. Ree crosses the yard. Dogs herald her arrival with loud barking.
You've got the wrong place, I expect. Who might you be? I'm Ree. My Dad's Jessup Dolly.
Merab gestures for Ree to come closer. towards the porch. Ree takes a few steps
You ain't here for trouble, are you? No, ma'am.
Cause one of my nephews is Buster Leroy, and didn't he shoot your daddy one time? Yes'm, but that ain't got nothing to do with me. They settled all that theirselves, I think.
Shooting him likely settled it. What is it you want? I got a real bad need to talk with Thump.
And he ain't got no need to talk to you.
Merab turns away towards her doorway.
Merab turns around to face Ree.
Some of our blood at least is the same. Ain't that s'posed to mean something' - isn't that what is always said?
Merab, irritated, moves to confront Ree.
Ain't you got no men could do this? No, Ma'am, I don't.
You go wait in the yard somewhere by that coop, and I'll tell Thump you're here.
Ree walks in the direction of the chicken coop. inside. Merab goes
EXT. THUMP MILTON'S YARD - AFTERNOON
Ree finds a place to sit next to the coop. The camera lingers on the various things Ree notices as she waits.
(to herself) Come on, already.
EXT. THUMP MILTON'S YARD - LATE AFTERNOON
Merab comes back outside carrying a mug and calls out to Ree.
He ain't likely to have time for you, child. I've got to talk to him. don't know, nobody does.
If he
Nope. Talking just causes witnesses. And he don't want for any of those. I'll wait.
You need to get yourself on home.
She gives Ree the mug.
Here, drink it down and be on your way.
Ree drinks and hands the empty mug back to Merab.
Thump knows you were in the valley, child. With Megan. And at Little Arthur's. He knows what you want to ask, and he don't want to hear it. And that's it? nothing to me?
He ain't gonna say
If you're listening, child, you got your answer.
Merab turns her back and walks toward her house. after her. Ree calls
Merab spins on her heel and gets up into Ree's face.
Don't you dare. Don't! I want you to listen to me, child. You need to turn around, and get yourself on home.
Merab turns and walks back to her house. doorstep, she turns back towards Ree. As she reaches the
Don't you make me come out here and tell you again!
Merab enters the house and slams the door. As Ree stomps away, Merab eyes her suspiciously from the window.
EXT. REE'S HOUSE - DAY
Ashlee bounces on the trampoline on a toy horse. Ree comes out onto the porch. Ashlee Dawn, come inside. Brownie and Cupcake. Bring Ashlee picks up the two horses and climbs off the trampoline.
INT. REE'S HOUSE - DAY
Ree is slicing potatoes into a sizzling pan. There is a loud knock at the door.
Ree, Get out here!
EXT. REE'S HOUSE, PORCH - DAY
Ree opens the door and steps out.
Do you know, there's people going 'round saying you best shut up. People you ought to listen to. Get your ass in the truck.
He grabs Ree's arm.
Don't touch me.
Get your ass in the truck.
Blond Milton pushes Ree towards the truck. Ree grabs the porch beam to resist. Blond Milton shoves her down the stairs.
Get in the truck!
Sonny comes out of the house.
Hey!
D'ruther I hit you, boy? Sonny, get back in the house. Cook them taters 'til they're brown then turn the stove off.
Don't hurt my sister. Sonny!
Blond Milton turns around, climbs the porch steps and grabs Sonny's collar.
Having balls is good, boy, but don't let 'em get you hurt. Sonny, get in the house!
(to Ree) Get your dumb ass in the truck. I'm not getting in there with you.
Get in the truck. There's something that you need to see.
Ree and Blond Milton get into the truck.
INT. BLOND MILTON'S TRUCK - DAY (DRIVING)
Jessup and me may have had our tussles, but he was my cousin. It's always a bad deal when these things blow. Where are we going?
Down the road. Down the road to where?
To someplace you need to see.
Ree anxiously looks around trying to get her bearings.
I/E. BLOND MILTON'S TRUCK - DAY
They drive past woodlands until they reach a burned out house. Blond Milton pulls off the road and turns off the engine.
INT. BLOND MILTON'S TRUCK - DAY (STATIONARY)
This right here is the last place me or anybody seen Jessup. He never blew no lab before.
I know it. But something must have jumped wrong this time. He's known for never fucking up labs or cooking bad batches. He's known for knowing what he's doing.
Well, you cook long enough, this is bound to happen. You saying Dad's up there burnt to a crisp?
That's what I'm saying. I'm going' up for a look.
Ree opens her door and starts to exit the truck. Blond Milton grabs her arm.
No! That shit's poison. It'll rip the skin off your bones! If Dad's up there, I'm bringing him home to bury.
Ree!
Ree wrests herself free and gets out of the truck, slamming the door behind her. Blond watches as she approaches the ruined house.
EXT. BURNED OUT HOUSE - DAY

Ree walks across the road to the burned out house. She cautiously approaches the house, looking at the details of the wreckage. One wall is blown out to the yard, windows have exploded and the blackened wall fragments and insulation dangle from the broken studs. She takes in a melted stove and notes weeds growing through the floorboards.
INT. BLOND MILTON'S TRUCK - DAY (DRIVING)
Ree and Blond Milton drive home silently. Ree looks out the window, lost in thought, weighing her options.
EXT. BLOND MILTON'S HOUSE - DAY
Blond Milton and Ree get out of the truck in front of his house. He stops Ree as she starts to walk home across the holler.
I know losing Jessup leaves you all hurting over there. I know it's a lot to handle. We'll make do.
Sonya and me talked about it. We feel we could take Sonny off your hands. Not Ashlee, I don't reckon, but we could take Sonny. You what?
We could take him and raise him up the rest of the way. My ass, you will.

We'd raise the boy way better'n you and that nut job mama of yours can. Maybe on down the line we'd take Ashlee, too. You go straight to hell, you son of a bitch. Sonny and Ashlee will die living in a cave with me and Mama before they spend one night with you.
Ree walks away, then turns back to Blond Milton.
Goddamn you! You must think I'm a stupid idiot. (MORE)
There's weeds growing chin high in that place. It must have been a year since that place blew!
Ree spits at Blond Milton's feet. He turns and leaves. angrily marches back across the holler to her house.
Ree EXT. REE'S YARD - DAY
Sonny and Ashlee place plastic bottles on a picnic table, then run back to where Ree is waiting. Ree props up a double-barrelled shotgun for Sonny and Ashlee to examine.
Ree puts the double-barreled gun on the ground and picks up the other shotgun that's lying there.

Now this gun is what you'll use when you're older. But this one is the gun I learned on. This is Daddy's squirrel gun. Now the most important thing is do not put your finger on the trigger unless you're ready to shoot, you're aimed at your target. And don't ever-- both of you look at me-- never point this at each other, not ever. Alright? Kneel down like you're praying. Yeah, just like that. Yup.
Sonny picks up the gun and rests it on his shoulder. Ree stands over him instructing him.
You see that cross? It's called the crosshairs. Aim that up at the middle of your target. And when that's lined up in the middle then you squeeze the trigger and you shoot.
Okay.
Ree! Hang on. (to Gail) Hey! (to Sonny and Ashlee) Both of you sit on your hands. Don't touch it.
Gail enters with her baby in a baby carrier.
Hey! Hey!
Damn, Sweet Pea. What's with all the guns? Just teaching them a little bit of survival.
Ree takes a peek at Ned in the carrier.
(to Ned) Hi!
Ree hugs her.
INT. REE'S HOUSE - DAY
Ree and Gail are standing at the kitchen counter. Ned is on the counter inside the carrier. Gail tosses a set of keys up onto the counter.
Got the truck off him. You are who I always did think you were, Darlin'. You truly are.
EXT. REE'S YARD - NIGHT
Gail and Ree come out of Ree's house. Gail carries Ned in his carrier. They open the doors of Floyd's truck and secure the carrier in place.
Turn it this way. Backwards?
Yup. Does your mom know what's going on? I'm not sure.
You think you should tell her? This is the exact shit she went crazy trying to get away from. It'd be too mean to tell her.
I guess she couldn't help, anyhow. Nope.
They climb into the truck and drive away.
INT. APRIL'S HOUSE, LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
A picking session is in process. MARIDETH, APRIL's mother, sings, accompanied by her friends playing music. April sits on the couch watching. In another part of the room, two couples play cards.
(sings) As I look on that valley far below. It's green as far as I can see. As my memories return, oh how my
For you and the days that used to be. High on a mountain, wind blowing
I wonder if you still remember me.
Marideth turns to her friends.
Play it through.
Ree and Gail (with baby carrier) knock at the door. April gets up to answer their knock.
(sings) I wonder if you still remember me Or has time erased your memory? As I listen to the breeze whisper
I wonder if you still remember me.
April opens the door and hesitates before greeting Ree.
(sings) Come all ye fair and tender ladies, Take a warning how you court your men.
Come on in.
She motions Ree and Gail in. They step inside quietly and survey the scene, checking to see if they know anyone.
It's Ma's birthday. Happy birthday.
You want anything? drink, food? I'm here huntin' Dad.
I was guessin' that. Come on in.
April leads Ree and Gail through the living room to her daughter's bedroom.
(sings) They're like a star on a a summer morning They first appear and then they're gone. I wish I was a little sparrow And I had wings and I could fly. I'd fly straight away...
INT. APRIL'S HOUSE - BEDROOM - NIGHT
In the cramped kids room, Gail and Ree pay close attention to April.
You know me'n Jessup quit keepin' company a good while ago. I figured. I thought you might still know a thing or two.
Well, I'm kind of afraid I might. I wish I could kick back for this one, Ree.
April sits down next to them.

No, no, I give that up. A couple months ago Jessup and me run across each other by total accident. He got me to laughing so, things rekindled for a day or two. Then he was gone again. Then about, maybe, three or four weeks back, I'd stopped at Cruikshank's, he was in there with three fellas I didn't know, and they didn't look to be having no fun, either. Dad say anything?
He looked square at me, but acted like he didn't know me, like he'd never seen me before. Something real wrong was going on.
Ree is lost in her thoughts trying to take this in. tries to look her in the eye.
I'm sorry.
EXT. WOODS - MORNING
Ree, Sonny and Ashlee sit silently with their back against a large oak. Ree holds a shotgun propped and ready to fire.
Sonny sneezes.
Bless you.
How long do we have to wait for a squirrel? A long time, usually.
Wish we could get deer. It's not time for deer.
When is it? In the morning or at night. know that.
(whispers to Ree) Do you see one? Mmm. Where?
Ashlee points.
(whispering) There. Ah! Help me pull the trigger. your finger right there.
Ree and Ashlee shoot.
EXT. REE'S YARD, SKINNING TABLE - MORNING
